(8) The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango - MongoDB - ODM

Before we start writing code, let's talk about what ODM and ORM actually are. We're going to use MongoDB's ODM, but it helps to understand the difference between these two first. In short, both act as a bridge between your code and the database, so you don't have to poke at the database directly.

This is the last chapter of MongoDB CRUD — Delete. And honestly, once you've gone through the Create chapter, Delete feels pretty easy. We'll cover deleteOne and deleteMany, plus one nasty gotcha that can wipe your entire collection in a single line.

This post walks through MongoDB's three update methods: updateOne, updateMany, and replaceOne, including the gotcha with $currentDate and the "full overwrite" trap in replaceOne.
